summary: After reviewing amniotic fluid embolism symptoms characterized by sudden onset shock within hours of childbirth, it strongly supports diagnosing the patient who experienced shock right after delivering vaginally due to the quick progression from apparent normal delivery to critical condition fitting these classic signs precisely. Key indicators include acute breathing difficulties followed swiftly by profound low blood pressure along with neurological changes potentially leading fatal outcomes if not promptly treated aggressively according to established emergency response guidelines emphasizing early intervention crucial survival chances improving significantly when acted upon rapidly recognizing warning signals
